rationale: The citing paper uses Sahni (2016) as a key prior result in its spillover framing, specifically noting that targeted advertising can create spillovers and that spillovers diminish with higher exposure. It then positions its own contribution as differing from prior spillover work by varying targeting precision and estimating spillovers to similar versus dissimilar products on an e-book platform. This is more than a perfunctory mention because the cited study helps motivate the paper's core research question about spillovers and targeting trade-offs.
relation_description: The citing paper extends Sahni's advertising spillover line of work to targeted promotions in a mobile e-book setting. It adds new evidence on crowding out of dissimilar products and opportunity costs when optimizing targeting objectives, rather than focusing only on advertiser-versus-competitor spillovers.
